description_long: This is a brown glass bottle with a long neck and a narrow mouth, designed in the style of a classic ale or beer bottle. The front features a prominent circular label with the word 'ALE' in bold white letters, surrounded by a decorative shield and crossed axes motif, giving it a rustic, medieval-inspired appearance. The label is bordered by wood-textured graphics that wrap around the bottle, simulating the look of wooden planks or barrels. The neck of the bottle has a smaller label with the word 'ALE' and a brown band, while the back is mostly covered with wood grain patterns and lacks any major branding or text. The bottle has a smooth, glossy finish, and the cap appears to be a standard crown cap, typical for beer bottles. There are no visible signs of damage, and the bottle appears clean and intact.
description: A brown glass ale bottle with a shield-and-axes label, wood-textured graphics, and a crown cap. The design is rustic and medieval-themed with a glossy finish.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main 'ALE' label with shield and axes, and the neck label.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the edge of the main label and the start of the wood-textured wrap.
description_view_2: Rear view dominated by wood grain graphics, with no branding or text visible.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the continuation of the wood-textured wrap and part of the shield motif.
attribute_label_or_branding: Front circular label with 'ALE' in white, shield and axes motif, brown neck label with 'ALE', wood-textured wrap, gold and black accents.
